intro: Capnophyllum peregrinum, also known as the 'straggly buttonbush', is a flowering plant species belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Capnophyllum peregrinum has been used to treat various health issues, with its reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Capnophyllum peregrinum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
